content.full_text:
SITE OF CALIFORNIA                                                EDMUND G. BROWN, JR., Governor

UNJFOLI TICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
99 K Street
[illegible] 95814
(c) 322-5660


                                                           October 3, 1975

                                                           75-180-137


Ms. Jean J. Strong
3971 North Angus Street
Fresno, California 93726

                                                Re: Request for Opinion
                                                    No. 75-180

Dear Ms. Strong:

     Thank you for your letter of September 19, 1975,
requesting an opinion with regard to the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. Sec-
tion 83114 of the Act authorizes an individual to
"request the Commission to issue an opinion with respect
to his duties under" the Act. (Emphasis added) your
letter does not indicate whether you are the candidate
with the potential conflict, and it therefore is not
possible to determine whether you are asking about your
duties under the Act. For this reason your request for
a formal opinion will be denied.

     It may be helpful to observe, however, that the
Political Reform Act does not prohibit an individual
from serving on the Board of Directors of an Irrigation
District merely because he is employed by a company with
which the District does business. The Act's prohibitions,
contained in Chapter 7, concern participating in making
or in any way attempting to use one's official position
to influence a governmental decision in which an individual
knows, or has reason to know, he has a financial interest.

     If you have any further questions, please contact
Ted Prin, a member of the Commission staff.

                                                Sincerely,

                                                [signature]
                                                Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                                Chairman, for the Commission

No. 156
Answered 9-22-75
Response Due 19-6-95

3971 N. Angeles St.
[illegible] 1975
Fresno, Ca. 93726

[illegible] N. [illegible]

[illegible] all these practices continue out of St. Said 532
Bernardino, Ca. 92401.

[illegible] Mr. [illegible]

I've ordered from Sears so many times and Sears has had no complaints on Sears, but is it a company with which to continue with. But no Desires? I'd like to know [illegible]

Yes, we have continued to operate [illegible] the store in El Paso. You may wish to compare with us on [illegible] future [illegible]

[illegible] Sears [illegible]

Sincerely,

Jean J. Strong

embedding.qa_text: October 3, 1975 Ms. Jean J. Strong 3971 North Angus Street Fresno, California 93726 Re: Request for Opinion No. 75-180 Dear Ms. Strong: Thank you for your letter of September 19, 1975, requesting an opinion with regard to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. Section 83114 of the Act authorizes an individual to "request the Commission to issue an opinion with respect to his duties under" the Act. (Emphasis added) Your letter does not indicate whether you are the candidate with the potential conflict, and it therefore is not possible to determine whether you are asking about your duties under the Act. For this reason your request for a formal opinion will be denied. It may be helpful to observe, however, that the Political Reform Act does not prohibit an individual from serving on the Board of Directors of an Irrigation District merely because he is employed by a company with which the District does business. The Act's prohibitions, contained in Chapter 7, concern participating in making or in any way attempting to use one's official position to influence a governmental decision in which an individual knows, or has reason to know, he has a financial interest. If you have any further questions, please contact Ted Prim, a member of the Commission staff. Sincerely, Daniel H. Lowenstein Daniel H. Lowenstein Chairman, for the Commission The handwritten letter is dated 19 Sept 75 and is addressed to David H. Lee, Esq., at the California State Bar, Sacramento, CA. The sender is Jean G. Strong from Fresno, CA. The content of the letter is as follows: --- 3971 N. Anasco St. Fresno, Ca 93726 19 Sept 1975 David H. Lee, Esq. California State Bar 925 J St Suite 522 Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Mr. Lee, In connection with the recent action taken by the District Attorney's Office in the District Court of Sacramento County, I am employed by a company which is involved in the District Court case. I would like to know what steps are to be taken to answer the Court Order. Sincerely, Jean G. Strong
